sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 44-year-old female with medical record number 113322, presents six weeks post-right medial unicompartmental arthroplasty.
She reports being "all right" and states that her knee is "good" with no problems, but mentions still experiencing numbness on the side of the knee.
The numbness is expected to decrease as the nerves heal.
The patient expresses satisfaction with the surgery and reports no pain.
Past medical history: Not mentioned.
Review of systems: Not mentioned.
Current medications: Not mentioned.
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient's gait was described as antalgic with a slight limp and decreased velocity.
She is not using an assistive device.
The right knee focused exam revealed a healed surgical scar, mild swelling, and tenderness on the area of the incision.
Range of motion is zero to 120 degrees, with everything else being normal.
Test Results: Radiographs of the right knee, taken today, show implants in a stable position with no evidence of loosening or early complications.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient is six weeks post-right medial unicompartmental arthroplasty with a stable implant and no evidence of complications.
Problem list: Right knee unicompartmental arthroplasty; numbness in the right knee; expected to resolve as nerves heal.
Plan:
I prescribed no new medications.
I instructed the patient to continue with physical therapy to work on strength, range of motion, and endurance.

I recommended avoiding lifting greater than 5-10 pounds, standing or sitting for more than 30 minutes, and advised against returning to work that involves labor-intensive activities for at least six weeks.
I also advised against driving until the patient feels safe to do so.
I ordered a note to excuse the patient from work for another six weeks.
I will follow up with the patient in six weeks via telemedicine to check on her progress.
Follow-up: The patient will return for a telemedicine visit in six weeks.
